_draft: Clinical content is a DRAFT skeleton — review and polish before forwarding externally.
synthesis: BP drifting up over the last 10 days (currently 146/92, was 132/84). Patient unsure when he last took Losartan. Coordinator follow-up scheduled for medication review and home cuff technique check.

turns.content: Hi David, this is Vardana. Your blood pressure this morning was 146 over 92 — a few points higher than last week, when you were running closer to 132 over 84. How are you feeling today?
turns.synthesis_snapshot: Opening greeting. BP drifting upward over 10 days.

turns.content: Feeling fine, just a little tired. Work's been hectic.
Got it. With your numbers drifting up, I want to make sure nothing has changed with your routine. When did you last take your Losartan?
Hmm, honestly I'm not sure. Maybe two days ago? I keep meaning to set up a pill reminder.

Thanks for being honest. Inconsistent dosing is a really common cause of the kind of drift we're seeing. I'm going to flag this for your coordinator so they can walk you through a routine that fits your week — we'll keep this at a watch level for now, not an urgent escalation, since you're feeling fine and your numbers aren't crisis-range.
turns.synthesis_snapshot: Adherence gap suspected. WATCH-level flag dispatched for coordinator review.

turns.content: Of course. Talk to you in a few days, David.
